summary: The proximal walls of a Class II cavity on a deciduous molar do not inherently converge toward the occlusal surface; instead, they generally align with the longitudinal axis of the tooth due to the structural organization of enamel rods. This alignment reflects standard dental anatomy but contradicts the specific assertion of convergence mentioned in the original conclusion.
